name: Decarbonising AI With Event-based Neural Networks
description:
Can an artificial neural network be energy efficient?

The artificial neural networks underlying modern AI are very computing intensive, taking a lot of energy to run. In contrast, the human brain has a power budget of only about 20 Watts. In this lecture, Thomas Nowotny from the University of Sussex will explore how spiking neural networks can offer a path in addressing the energy issues that face modern AI systems. Learn about the results of his research in the field deploying trained networks on the Intel Loihi 2 neuromorphic system. Including reflections on interpreting interactions with AI systems.

name: Water Bankruptcy: Tracing Water through Food
description:
What if water could speak, what stories would it tell?

Would it speak of growing food? Or of water bankruptcy? Of being pulled from the earth faster than it can return. This workshop invites participants to explore the idea of Water Bankruptcy, examining how these hidden imbalances shape our food systems, environments and everyday lives. 

We begin with a shared reading, inviting multiple ways of seeing and interpreting water. Then, moving through CALORIE, we trace water through food systems, uncovering the idea of Virtual Water.

Through making puppets, explore touch, reflection, imagination and storytelling. We invite you to give shape to your experiences, transforming memory and observation into characters and narratives that can be held, shared and reimagined.

name: Fixed or Fluid? On Labour and Essentialism
description:
Do you hate labels?

To essentialise is to claim that certain qualities are true of certain groups, such as the idea that women are naturally more caring or that Indian food is always spicy. But are these evident and natural facts true?

In this workshop we will explore how identities and what is essential to them are shifting categories, particularly in contexts like nutrition, food production, domestic work, and agriculture. Rather than offering fixed answers, this workshop encourages curiosity, reflection, and dialogue. Participants will leave with new tools to recognize and challenge essentialist ideas, and to see labour—and identity—not as static or natural, but as fluid, dynamic, and shaped by history, power, and context.

Come explore 'CALORIE', identities and the ‘essence’ that have come to define them over time.

name: Mayday Melody: Labour Songs
description:
What do you listen to when you work? 

Across time and cultures, labour has often found expression in song. Songs tell us a lot about the people who sing them and how their lives and labour shape rhythm and lyrics. 

From paddy fields in Karnataka to the courtyards of Maharashtra, this session explores the rich themes of labour through music. What does our music say about us? Does work flavour the composition of our tunes? Join us to listen to music and sing along as we explore labour through music.

name: Fat, Sugar, Salt: The Inner Working of Cells
description:
Do cells distinguish between the nutrients they get? What do they do to process different nutrients? What is metabolism at a molecular level, and how does the cell's metabolism determine its fate? 

In this talk, we'll explore the inner workings of cells and how they process different nutrients. We'll explore how 'energy' is made or stored, and why different nutrients result in very different outcomes for cells. The talk will end with how cells are now being used to solve the 'supply' problems for many foods - from milk proteins, to other nutrients.

name: Our Plates Across Time: Tracing Food Then, Now, and Next
description:
The grains that once fed Bengaluru are slowly being replaced by code, quietly changing what and how we eat.
What if what you crave isn’t entirely your choice, but shaped by your body, your surroundings, even invisible microbes?
Join Thejaswini as she walks you through stories of changing diets, disappearing diversity, and the systems behind what ends up on our plate.
Careful—this walkthrough might linger long after your next meal.
